hero.title: What's buzzing in our Holter?
hero.subtitle: See how we built the precision.

spec.title: Custom printed circuit board.
spec.desc: We designed our own PCB to optimise power consumption and minimise ECG signal interference. The advanced analogue front-end precisely amplifies the patient's chest potential difference, filtering noise and delivering a clean cardiac signal.

anatomy.title: Device anatomy.
anatomy.subtitle: Key integrated circuits and modules implemented in Rythmio.

items.title: Microcontroller
items.desc: ESP32-WROOM-32E is the heart of the device. This dual-core chip coordinates sample collection, manages memory and handles Bluetooth Low Energy.

items.title: Analogue Front-End
items.desc: Medical-grade, 24-bit ADS1292 converter. Analogue ground and power supply are rigorously separated from digital to eliminate noise.

items.title: Real-Time Clock
items.desc: RTC with independent backup precisely synchronises time, so every file on the SD card carries an accurate timestamp.

items.title: Ultra-Low Power Display
items.desc: E-Ink display continuously shows heart rate (BPM), battery level and a wireless connection icon.

items.title: Offline Storage (SD Card)
items.desc: Integrated microSD slot enables continuous recording of raw ECG signal, ensuring patient data safety.

items.title: Piezoelectric Buzzer
items.desc: Acts as a safety assistant. Beeps on electrode disconnection detection and emits a signal after a successful session.

items.title: Power & Charging
items.desc: A large 3500 mAh Li-Ion cell provides multi-day operation. The circuit integrates a USB-C port with overcharge protection.

items.title: EMI Filtering & ESD
items.desc: Passive EMI/RFI filtering and dedicated diodes protect sensitive electronics and the patient from electrostatic discharges.

items.title: Motion Correlation
items.desc: Built-in accelerometer converts acceleration into physical activity, allowing differentiation of exertion heart rate from motion anomalies.

interface.title: Physical interface operation.
interface.subtitle: The device has two physical function buttons located on the right side of the enclosure. Their behaviour depends on the current device state.

items.label: Both together
items.text: Confirm parameters and start the signal recording procedure.

items.label: Top button
items.text: Wake the E-Ink display to verify current parameters.

items.label: Bottom button (Event Marker)
items.text: Mark an important moment (patient event in case of discomfort) for later review.

items.label: Both together
items.text: Manually wake the Bluetooth module and initialise wireless connection with the app.

gallery.subtitle: From first sketches and 3D models to component assembly. Grab and drag to see more.
